walkthroughs.title: OpenAI Manager Setup
walkthroughs.description: A complete beginner-friendly guide to setting up the OpenAI Manager for local model orchestration. Covers Node.js installation, cloning the repo, dependency setup, API key management via environment variables, model preference configuration, and launching the local dashboard.
steps: Install Node.js 18+ and npm from https://nodejs.org/
Run `npm install` inside the project folder

walkthroughs.title: Proot Sandbox Management
walkthroughs.description: Create and manage isolated Linux sandboxes with Proot for secure development on Android or low-privilege hosts. Covers install, login, toolchain setup, directory binds, and cleanup.
steps: Install tools: `apk add nodejs python3 git curl vim`
Exit with `exit`; remove with `proot-distro remove alpine`

walkthroughs.title: GGUF v3 Quantization: Q4_0 / Q4_K / Q5_K / Q8_0
walkthroughs.description: Deep dive into GGUF v3 quantization formats: block layouts, bit budgets, accuracy trade-offs, and exact commands to convert to FP16 then quantize to Q4_0, Q4_K_M, Q5_K_M, Q8_0. Includes perplexity benchmarking.
steps: Pick Q4_K_M for balance, Q8_0 for quality, Q4_0 for edge devices

walkthroughs.title: Install All Code-Assist CLIs in One Place
walkthroughs.description: One-shot setup for Slack CLI, JetBrains Toolbox, Aider, GitHub CLI, Ollama, ripgrep, fzf, and lazygit. Covers macOS, Linux, and Windows install paths plus useful shell aliases.
steps: Install a package manager (Homebrew / winget / apt / apk)
Slack CLI: `brew install slackcorp/slack/slack` or see Slack docs
GitHub CLI: `brew install gh` / `winget install GitHub.cli` / `apt install gh`
Productivity CLIs: `brew install ripgrep fzf lazygit jq`
Add aliases: `alias ai='aider'`, `alias lg='lazygit'`, `alias oll='ollama run llama3.1'`

walkthroughs.title: Aider + Ollama: Local AI Pair Programming
walkthroughs.description: Set up Aider to use a local Ollama model for private, offline AI pair programming. Covers Ollama install, pulling a coding model, launching Aider with the local endpoint, and everyday commands for asking questions, editing files, and committing changes.
steps: Pull a coding model: `ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:1.5b` (or `llama3.1`, `codellama`, `deepseek-coder-v2`)
Verify the model runs: `ollama run qwen2.5-coder:1.5b` — then type `/bye` to exit
Open your project repo in a terminal.
Add files to the chat: `/add src/myfile.py`
Ask for changes: `refactor this function to use async/await`
Review diffs, accept with `/commit`, or undo with `/undo`
Pro tip: add `export OLLAMA_API_BASE=http://localhost:11434` to your shell rc if Aider can't find Ollama.

about.title: Aegis Foundry — Living ASCII Dashboard
about.description: A sovereign, auto-updating terminal-style dashboard for tracking projects, mastering workflows, and surfacing the most important next steps. Built as a static GitHub Pages site with a Python data generator, rainbow ASCII art, and expandable walkthroughs.
features: Live ASCII headline renderer with rainbow hue cycling
GitHub project tracker with next-step focus
Curated technical walkthroughs (desktop + terminal + FAQ style)
Forum scaffold with localStorage post persistence

seo.description: Terminal-first guides to install Claude Code CLI, Aider, Ollama, GGUF quantization, and every tool you actually need. Daily ASCII flex + foundry forums.

daily_flex.title: ASCII Sine-Wave of the Day
daily_flex.description: A deterministic sine wave rendered in ASCII, seeded by today's date. Refreshes once per day at 00:00 UTC.

workflow_cards.name: Claude Code CLI
workflow_cards.tagline: The most-used coding assistant in the terminal.
workflow_cards.why_use: Claude Code is Anthropic's official terminal agent. You describe what you want in plain English and it reads, edits, tests, and commits code from your shell. It is the fastest way to refactor, debug, scaffold, and explore a codebase without leaving the keyboard.

metrics.evidence: Highest mindshare among terminal-first AI coding tools; recommended by Aider/Cursor power-users for agentic shell workflows.

dependencies.name: Node.js 18+
dependencies.why: Claude Code is distributed as an npm package

dependencies.name: Git
dependencies.why: Claude Code uses git context for safer edits

dependencies.name: Anthropic API key
dependencies.why: Claude Code bills against your Anthropic account

install_methods.name: npm global — cross-platform
install_methods.pitfalls: If you see 'npm ERR! EACCES', configure a global npm prefix or use the npx fallback below.

install_methods.name: npx one-shot — no global install
steps: # Run the latest version without installing
install_methods.pitfalls: Slower startup because npx downloads/checks the package. Corporate proxies may block npx.

install_methods.name: Windows PowerShell + winget
pre_steps: # If Node is missing, install LTS:
# Refresh PATH in this session without restarting:
install_methods.pitfalls: PATH does not update until you open a new PowerShell window unless you refresh $env:Path manually.
install_methods.fallback: Download Node.js from https://nodejs.org/en/download, install, then npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

install_methods.name: WSL2 Ubuntu / Debian bash
pre_steps: # Install a modern Node if system node is old:
install_methods.pitfalls: Ubuntu's default nodejs package may be older than 18. Use the NodeSource curl pipe shown above if needed.
install_methods.fallback: curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.7/install.sh | bash && source ~/.bashrc && nvm install 20 && npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

install_methods.name: macOS Homebrew
install_methods.pitfalls: Homebrew on Apple Silicon installs to /opt/homebrew; make sure it is in your PATH.

dependency_install.name: Install Node.js LTS on Windows (no admin: use nvm-windows)

use_cases.title: Scaffold a feature from a blank terminal
use_cases.context: You just created an empty folder and want a working feature without touching an editor.
demo_commands: > Create a minimal Python Flask app with one route '/' that returns JSON {status: 'ok'}
# Claude will show planned files. Accept each with 'y'.
use_cases.expected_output: app.py appears, requirements are inferred, and the server starts on http://127.0.0.1:5000.

use_cases.title: Fix a failing test
use_cases.context: You ran the test suite and one test fails with a confusing error.
demo_commands: # Copy the error, then launch Claude Code in the project root:
> Test X failed with 'Cannot find module ./helpers'. Diagnose and fix.
# Review the diff Claude proposes, accept with 'y'.
use_cases.expected_output: The missing import or path is corrected and the test passes.

use_cases.title: Refactor a large file into modules
use_cases.context: src/app.js is 600 lines and you want it split into src/lib/ modules with tests.
demo_commands: > Refactor src/app.js into smaller modules under src/lib/ and add unit tests for the extracted helpers.
use_cases.expected_output: New files appear under src/lib/, src/app.js shrinks, and tests are added.

use_cases.title: Understand a foreign codebase
use_cases.context: You cloned a repo and need a 30-second orientation before contributing.
demo_commands: > What does this repo do, what are the main entry points, and how do I run it?
use_cases.expected_output: A concise summary of the project purpose, key files, and run commands.

pro_tips: Run Claude Code inside a git repo so every change is reversible with git checkout -- .
Type /compact when the conversation gets long; Claude will summarize context.
Type /cost to see estimated API usage for the session.
In CI/automation, restrict tools with claude-code --allowedTools edit,bash,view
Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in your shell profile or load it from a .env file.
